title: Dispatches from the Substrate — Essays · Nirodha Collective

items.title: The Trauma That Still Runs in Us / Das Trauma, das noch in uns läuft
items.content_text: Fear touches no one and still shapes the whole body: fear alone lowers the number of offspring and hardens into the worldview we call realism. We carry the nervous system of a hunted animal — and are the first that can recognise it and calm it.

items.title: We Are the Meteorite / Wir sind der Meteorit
items.content_text: Five times an executioner came from outside — asteroid, volcano, poisoned sea. The sixth extinction has no external cause but a principle: us. And we are the first projectile in Earth’s history that can decide, in mid-flight, not to become an impact.

items.title: The Mistake That Eats Itself / Der Fehler, der sich selbst frisst
items.content_text: Devouring is a late strategy, not the ground of life — and as the order of everything it is malignant. Nuclear arsenals and the sixth extinction are not a test still pending: they are the running finding.

items.title: The Fermi Paradox That Isn't One / Das Fermi-Paradoxon, das keines ist
items.content_text: Not extinct — in quarantine. A mature cosmos keeps a predator-contaminated civilisation at arm’s length. The sky is not silent by accident; the door opens from the inside, once we overcome the principle.
